title: How to Listen with Intention
author: Michael J. Chase
subline: The foundation of true connection, communication, and relationships.
customHTML:
<svg viewBox="0 0 1600 2400" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid slice" style="position:absolute;inset:0;width:100%;height:100%">
  <!-- Two speech bubbles facing each other. The left bubble is a talker (dense text). The right bubble is a listener (almost empty, just a single red dot — the 'intention' of what was heard). -->
  <g transform="translate(800 1700)" fill="none">
    <!-- Left speech bubble: talker -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2" fill="none">
      <rect x="-660" y="-220" width="500" height="280"/>
      <path d="M -340 60 L -300 100 L -380 60 Z"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Lines of talker text (15 hairlines) -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1.5">
      <line x1="-620" y1="-180" x2="-200" y2="-180"/>
      <line x1="-620" y1="-140" x2="-260" y2="-140"/>
      <line x1="-620" y1="-100" x2="-220" y2="-100"/>
      <line x1="-620" y1="-60" x2="-280" y2="-60"/>
      <line x1="-620" y1="-20" x2="-200" y2="-20"/>
      <line x1="-620" y1="20" x2="-260" y2="20"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Right speech bubble: listener (almost empty) -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="2" fill="none">
      <rect x="160" y="-220" width="500" height="280"/>
      <path d="M 340 60 L 300 100 L 380 60 Z"/>
    </g>
    <!-- The single red dot in the listener bubble -->
    <circle cx="410" cy="-80" r="14" fill="#fc1c46"/>
    <!-- A few faint listening markers -->
    <g stroke="#4c4c4c" stroke-width="1" opacity="0.4">
      <line x1="220" y1="-180" x2="380" y2="-180"/>
      <line x1="220" y1="-140" x2="340" y2="-140"/>
      <line x1="220" y1="0" x2="360" y2="0"/>
      <line x1="220" y1="20" x2="320" y2="20"/>
    </g>
  </g>
  <text x="800" y="2200" text-anchor="middle" font-family="'Sui','Söhne','Inter',system-ui,sans-serif" font-size="14" font-weight="500" fill="#4c4c4c" letter-spacing="0.4em">HEAR WHAT WAS SAID, NOT JUST SPOKEN</text>
</svg>
rationale: Two speech bubbles facing each other. The left bubble (the talker) is full of dense hairline text. The right bubble (the listener) is mostly empty — just a single red dot at its center. That's 'intention': you don't memorize the words, you carry the essence. Title 280px mixed case, three lines, weight 300.
